data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated against the AS-SHIPPED code. VERDICT: clean pairing — NO G4 cross-child finding.

This audit confirms the WITHDRAWAL of the recurring prior G4 finding on this ticket (the "labels_applied list-vs-int representation conflict" between 2359's count-canary and 33d0's surviving applier). That finding was grounded in pre-collapse code that 33d0 has since deleted; it is no longer load-bearing.

REPO RE-VERIFICATION (current HEAD):
- 33d0 (closed) collapsed the dual path: apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update is now a thin delegator to _mutation_to_batch_dict + update_one; the old list-valued `labels_applied: list[str]` payload key is GONE (search of apply_outbound.py finds only a docstring mention at L139, no list assignment).
- 2359 (closed) mints all per-sub-op counts FRESH as INTEGERS on the single surviving batch path: batch_dispatch.py L496 (`_labels_computed = _labels_applied = 0`), L515 (`_labels_applied += 1`), L589-598 fills the `subop_applied: dict[str, int]` out-param with labels/comments/links _computed and _applied, all ints. applier.py L721 threads `_subop` (int dict), L803-805 surfaces all three uniformly as ints (default 0).
- The canary predicate (applier.py L814-819) is `_subop.get(f"{kind}_computed",0) > 0 and _subop.get(f"{kind}_applied",0) == 0` over ("labels","comments","links") — WELL-TYPED over all three because every operand is an int. The `[] != 0` / truthiness hazard the prior finding posited is structurally impossible in the shipped design.
- The dedup false-positive concern (raised under G6) is resolved: `links_computed` is incremented AFTER the already-present skip (batch_dispatch.py L574-578), so an idempotent re-sync is computed==0 and does NOT fire the canary.

7-MODE WALK (this pairing):
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 2359 and 33d0 co-edit batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py, but 2359 declares `depends_on 33d0` (deps confirm; description "Sequencing: depends on story D … so the counts come from one path"). 33d0 collapsed to ONE path first; 2359 instrumented the single survivor. Surviving code is internally consistent (integer `_subop` accumulator threads from update_one into the single outcome). No unguarded hazard.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. The previously-flagged labels representation conflict is gone; 2359's count-based canary assumes integer counts and the surviving applier produces integer counts for ALL THREE kinds. The cited "parity with inbound links_applied" (an int, apply_inbound.py) is now an accurate model for every kind.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 2359's only input dep (story D/33d0) is declared via depends_on; it needs no schema/fixture input from 8937/fe57 (instruments an existing dispatch loop), so the absence of those edges is appropriate.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 2359 (computed>0 && applied==0 canary), 33d0 (combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect), 822a (bidirectional round-trip) are distinct intents over the same production path. 2359 declares applier consolidation (→33d0) and probe wiring (→822a) OUT consistently; 822a round-trips comments/links/parent and asserts inbound↔outbound outcomes, not the `*_applied` telemetry payload, so it imposes no expectation on 2359's counters.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The 33d0→2359 ordering is captured by depends_on 33d0; collapse-then-instrument is the correct sequence, both closed in that order.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The added outcome keys + RECON-line sub-op fields (applier.py L865-869) are purely additive (non-update actions carry no sub-ops, default 0). 2359 AC5 asserts the count invariant + outcome flags, not specific log strings (one structural caplog WARNING check is required by the warn-first AC3). No brittle downstream expectation imposed.
- Residual references — BENIGN. 2359's counters/canary/warn-first flag are additive plumbing on the surviving batch path; they reference no part of the deleted typed leaf; the cited "parity" is with apply_inbound's intact links_applied.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 coverage matter (owned by story B/8937 per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 2359): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign. Findings list is EMPTY.
